label: PRJ-015 UI usability batch build-closure (REQ-131..138 / PI-172..179)

session.session_title: PRJ-015 UI usability enhancements — planning & requirements
session.session_executive_summary: Delivered the eight PRJ-015 desktop-UI usability enhancements: an owning-entity column on the Fields grid and entity name in the field detail, a dedicated fields grid on the entity detail, hover-to-reveal full text on truncating read-only fields with a wrapping project purpose, a client-side search box on every list panel, a filterable/collapsible sidebar, inline lifecycle status actions, and a back/forward cross-record navigation trail. Built on branch pi-ui-usability (seven feature commits, full UI suite 992 passed), merged to main, with PI-172..179 resolved.
session.session_description: Design + build session for the PRJ-015 UI usability batch.

conversation.conversation_title: Desktop UI usability enhancements — requirements definition
conversation.conversation_purpose: Define the requirements for the eight desktop-UI usability enhancements so each traces to the human conversation that established it.
conversation.conversation_description: Doug specified four field/entity/project-detail improvements; four broader themes were added; all eight were worded as requirements and then built and delivered.
conversation.conversation_summary: Eight enhancements agreed, built, tested (UI suite 992 passed), and merged to main; PI-172..179 resolved.

commits.commit_message_first_line: feat(v2-ui): reveal full text on truncating read-only fields; wrap project purpose (REQ-134/PI-175)
commits.commit_message_full:
feat(v2-ui): reveal full text on truncating read-only fields; wrap project purpose (REQ-134/PI-175)

read_only_line now mirrors its full value into the tooltip so any short
read-only field reveals its content on hover across all panels;
read_only_text wraps to widget width and tooltips too. Project detail's
purpose field switches to the wrapping read_only_text so it no longer
truncates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: feat(v2-ui): show owning entity on Fields grid + name in field detail (REQ-131,132/PI-172,173)
commits.commit_message_full:
feat(v2-ui): show owning entity on Fields grid + name in field detail (REQ-131,132/PI-172,173)

The Fields list gains an Entity column populated with each field's owning
entity name (resolved on the worker thread via one bulk references read +
one entities read, with a soft-delete stash fallback). The field detail
view's parent-entity row now shows the entity name together with its
identifier instead of the bare identifier.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: feat(v2-ui): dedicated fields grid on the entity detail (REQ-133/PI-174)
commits.commit_message_full:
feat(v2-ui): dedicated fields grid on the entity detail (REQ-133/PI-174)

Adds an EntityFieldsGridSection (a third GridContract over the shared grid,
mirroring WorkTaskGridSection) listing each of an entity's fields with
identifier, name, data type, required flag, and status. The entities panel
joins the inbound field_belongs_to_entity edges to the field records on the
worker thread and renders the grid above References. Both entity-detail
grids get distinct object names so callers can disambiguate.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: feat(v2-ui): client-side search box on every list panel (REQ-135/PI-176)
commits.commit_message_full:
feat(v2-ui): client-side search box on every list panel (REQ-135/PI-176)

ListDetailPanel gains a debounced toolbar search (LinkFilterInput) that
narrows the master list across the visible columns of the already-loaded
records — zero backend, propagates to every table-backed panel. _records
is now the filtered view of _all_records; the status label shows
"N of M records" while filtering; navigation clears an active search so a
hidden target stays reachable. TopicsPanel (tree model) and ReferencesPanel
(own filter strip) opt out via _search_enabled=False.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(v2-ui): sidebar filter + collapsible groups (REQ-136/PI-177)

The sidebar gains filter_entries() (hide non-matching entries, and headers
whose entries all hide) and clickable group headers that collapse/expand
their entries; an active filter overrides collapse so matches always show.
A "Filter navigation…" box above the sidebar list (in the sidebar column,
both engagement and non-engagement layouts) drives it. Headers are now
enabled-but-not-selectable so clicks toggle without ever taking selection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commits.commit_message_first_line: feat(v2-ui): inline lifecycle status actions from the list (REQ-137/PI-178)
commits.commit_message_full:
feat(v2-ui): inline lifecycle status actions from the list (REQ-137/PI-178)

ListDetailPanel gains a _status_action_spec hook + _append_status_menu helper
that adds a "Set status" context-menu submenu of valid next states, applied
without opening the edit dialog. Wired for Sessions (patch_session) and
Planning Items (update_planning_item), each offering only the transitions its
lifecycle vocab permits; other panels can opt in by implementing the hook.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(v2-ui): back/forward cross-record navigation trail (REQ-138/PI-179)

Following a reference (the navigation router) now records the originating
(sidebar entry, selected identifier) on a back stack; a new Go menu with
Back/Forward actions (Alt+Left / Alt+Right) steps through the trail and
returns the user to where they came from. A fresh jump clears the forward
stack; actions enable/disable with stack state. Inbound references were
already one-click navigable via the references grid.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
